content.full_text:
A-81-12-504

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660             322-5901            322-6441              322-6444

December 23, 1981


Dennis A. Lee
City Attorney
City of Martinez
525 Henrietta Street
Martinez, CA 94553

Re: Your Request for Written Advice on Behalf of Jim Thelen,
    Martinez City Council Member

Dear Mr. Lee:

     Thank you for you recent letter requesting written
advice from the Commission pursuant to Government Code Section
83114(b).1/  As you state them, the facts and your question
are as follows:

     One of our Councilmembers is a lawyer and shares
office space with another lawyer [Al Turnbaugh]. They
have totally separate operations; share a secretary;
share books, but each pays their own expenses and do
not share income. The Councilmember's practice is in
criminal law. The other attorney's practice is in
business law. The other attorney appeared before the
Council with his client, advocating a proposed sub-
division. The opponents to this subdivision alleged
that the Councilmember cannot participate in the decision
as to this proposed subdivision because of his relation-
ship with this attorney. It was also brought out by
the opponents to the subdivision that the two attorneys
shared ownership in a building. The building has been
renovated and they both share a debt and rent out space
in this building to meet the cost of the debt (loan).
Their offices are located in this building.

     My initial opinion as to this set of facts was
that the Councilmember had no conflict of interest, but
there was an appearance of the conflict and therefore

_____________________
1/  All statutory references made are to the Government
Code unless otherwise noted.

Dennis A. Lee
December 23, 1981
Page Two


should refrain from participation in this matter.
Subsequently, the subdivision was rejected and the
attorney for this subdivision has asked for recon-
sideration of the decision because he alleges that
there is no conflict of interest nor appearance of a
conflict of interest with the Councilmember who also
shares offices with him and owns the building jointly
with him.

     The issue would seem to be, does the Councilmember
who owns a building jointly with this lawyer have to
refrain from participation whenever the lawyer (who
shares office space and owns a building with him) appears
before the Council on behalf of a client?  It is assumed
that the debt that they share and the rental they receive
from this property exceeds $1,000.00.

     Your question is similar to that which we addressed in
our April 13, 1981, advice letter to the Honorable Byron
Sher (copy attached).  There, we concluded that an appraiser
who shared office expenses, but not income, would not be
required to disqualify himself with respect to a decision
affecting clients of his associates.

     Under the Sher analysis, in the absence of profit-sharing,
clients of other lawyers are not a source of income to Mr. Thelen
and he need not disqualify himself as to them, unless there
is some other financial involvement between Mr. Thelen and
those clients.  As to Mr. Turnbaugh, we likewise conclude
that he is not a source of income to Mr. Thelen as a result
of the office sharing arrangement.

     However, in Mr. Thelen's case, there are two differences
from the Sher situation which must also be addressed.  First,
in addition to the office sharing arrangement, Mr. Thelen
and the other attorney jointly own a building.  They jointly
share the building debt and rents.  In addition, Mr. Turnbaugh
represents his clients before the City Council.  In the Sher
letter, that was not the issue presented.

     The mere sharing of ownership does not cause one owner
to become a source of income to the other.  Absent specific
facts which indicate that Mr. Turnbaugh is a source of income

Dennis A. Lee
December 23, 1981
Page Three


to Mr. Thelen, such as some other financial relationship
existing between them, which is not disclosed by the facts
as stated in your letter, there is no conflict of interest
under the Political Reform Act.2/ Consequently, we need not
address the issue of whether a decision affecting one of
Mr. Turbaugh's clients would have a material financial
effect on Mr. Turbaugh.

     If you have any questions, please feel free to contact
me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Robert E. Beidigh
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

REL:km

Enclosure:  Letter to Byron Sher dated April 13, 1981




_________________________

2/  Government Code Sections 81000-91014.  Keep in mind
that the Commission's advice always is limited to only the
provisions of the Political Reform Act and does not cover
other possible legal issues.

City of Martinez

525 HENRIETTA STREET • MARTINEZ
CALIFORNIA 94553 • (415) 372-

December 9, 1981


State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Ms. Barbara Milman, Chief of Leral Division

Dear Ms. Milman:

I have been requested to seek an opinion from the Fair
Political Practices Commission pursuant to Government Code
Section 83114(b) with reference to an alleged or possible con-
flict of interest as to one of our Councilmembers. The facts
of the situation are as such:

One of our Councilmembers is a lawyer and shares office
space with another lawyer. They have totally separate opera-
tions; share a secretary; share books, but each pays their
own expenses and do not share income. The Councilmember's
practice is in criminal law. The other attorney's practice is
in business law. The other attorney appeared before the Council
with his client, advocating a proposed subdivision. The oppo-
nents to this subdivision alleged that the Councilmember can-
not participate in the decision as to this proposed subdivision
because of his relationship with this attorney. It was also
brought out by the opponents to the subdivision that the two
attorneys shared ownership in a building. The building has been
renovated and they both share a debt and rent out space in this
building to meet the cost of the debt (loan). Their offices are
located in this building.

My initial opinion as to this set of facts was that the
Councilmember had no conflict of interest, but there was an
appearance of the conflict and therefore should refrain from
participation in this matter. Subsequently, the subdivision was
rejected and the attorney for this subdivision has asked for re-
consideration of the decision because he alleges that there is
no conflict of interest nor appearance of a conflict of interest
with the Councilmember who also shares offices with him and owns
the building jointly with him.

-2-

The issue would seem to be, does the Councilmember who
owns a building jointly with this lawyer have to refrain
from participation whenever the lawyer (who shares office
space and owns a building with him) appears before the Council
on behalf of a client? It is assumed that the debt that they
share and the rental they receive from this property exceeds
$1,000.00.

An expedited opinion would be greatly appreciated, as a
decision on the reconsideration issue will have to be made
shortly. Thank you for your assistance in this matter and
please feel free to call me at the following numbers if you
should desire further information. (415) 372-4927 or 934-6050.

Very truly yours,

Dennis L. Lee
City Attorney

DAL:bs

sections.question: Does the Councilmember who owns a building jointly with this lawyer have to refrain from participation whenever the lawyer (who shares office space and owns a building with him) appears before the Council on behalf of a client? It is assumed that the debt that they share and the rental they receive from this property exceeds $1,000.00.
sections.conclusion: Absent specific facts which indicate that Mr. Turnbaugh is a source of income to Mr. Thelen, such as some other financial relationship existing between them, which is not disclosed by the facts as stated in your letter, there is no conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act. Consequently, we need not address the issue of whether a decision affecting one of Mr. Turnbaugh's clients would have a material financial effect on Mr. Turnbaugh.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city councilmember who is a lawyer and jointly owns a building with another attorney must disqualify himself from voting on matters where that attorney appears as counsel before the council.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the councilmember need not disqualify himself under the Political Reform Act because the other attorney is not a source of income to the councilmember merely through the office sharing and building co-ownership arrangements, absent other undisclosed financial relationships.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated December 9, 1981) and the FPPC response letter (dated December 23, 1981). Clear question and conclusion sections present. Reference made to prior Sher advice letter (April 13, 1981). Minor formatting variations in original document but all key content preserved.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does the Councilmember who owns a building jointly with this lawyer have to refrain from participation whenever the lawyer (who shares office space and owns a building with him) appears before the Council on behalf of a client? It is assumed that the debt that they share and the rental they receive from this property exceeds $1,000.00.

CONCLUSION: Absent specific facts which indicate that Mr. Turnbaugh is a source of income to Mr. Thelen, such as some other financial relationship existing between them, which is not disclosed by the facts as stated in your letter, there is no conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act. Consequently, we need not address the issue of whether a decision affecting one of Mr. Turnbaugh's clients would have a material financial effect on Mr. Turnbaugh.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responded to a request regarding whether a Martinez city councilmember who shares office space and jointly owns a building with another attorney must recuse himself from voting on matters where that attorney appears before the council. The FPPC found no conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act absent evidence of direct income flow between the councilmember and the attorney.
